title: Issue Breakdown: Decompose Into Epics + Child Issues
description:
Using the Spec (mcq-7h6) as input, decompose the refactor into bd epics and child issues. Each child issue must be small, independently implementable, and have acceptance criteria.

Suggested epic areas:
- Data Layer: Extract scenario data management, define data contracts
- UI Components: Refactor/split components, establish patterns
- State Management: Replace monolithic GameContext, introduce proper state architecture
- Testing Infrastructure: Unit tests, integration tests, E2E setup
- Accessibility: a11y audit, ARIA, keyboard nav, screen reader support
- Build Tooling: TypeScript config, linting rules, CI pipeline

Each child issue should:
- Be completable in a single session (< 4 hours of work)
- Have clear acceptance criteria
- Specify dependencies on other issues where ordering matters
- Include a time estimate
acceptance_criteria:
1. Epics created in bd (type epic) for each major area identified in the Spec
2. Each epic has child task issues (created with --parent)
3. Every child issue has acceptance criteria and a time estimate
4. Dependency links set between issues where ordering matters
5. bd graph produces a readable dependency graph of the full refactor
6. No issue is larger than ~4 hours of estimated work

title: Spec: Write Refactor Specification
description:
Synthesize Research and Tech Stack findings into a refactor specification. Define the target architecture, modularization plan, and acceptance criteria per major area.

This is the central planning document that Issue Breakdown will use to generate epics and child issues.

Areas to cover:
- Target state management approach (replace monolithic GameContext with what?)
- Service layer boundaries and API contracts
- Component hierarchy and reuse strategy
- Directory structure / module boundaries
- Migration strategy (incremental vs. big-bang, feature-flag approach)
- Acceptance criteria for each major area (data layer, UI components, state mgmt, testing, a11y, build tooling)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Target architecture defined (state management approach, service layer boundaries, component hierarchy)
2. Modularization plan documented (what moves where, new directory structure)
3. Acceptance criteria written for each major area (data layer, UI components, state management, testing, a11y, build tooling)
4. Migration strategy outlined (incremental vs. big-bang, feature-flag approach)
5. Spec reviewed and approved before Issue Breakdown begins

title: Research: Understand Current POC Behavior
description:
Audit the current mcqueen POC to document user flows, component boundaries, data flow through GameContext, service responsibilities, and known pain points. Produce a written artifact that the Spec issue can reference.

Key areas to cover:
- All 7 pages (Market, PlayerDetail, Portfolio, Watchlist, Mission, Leaderboard, Timeline) and their interactions
- The 3 demo scenarios (midweek, live, playoffs) and how they drive behavior
- GameContext.jsx as the single state management hub
- Services layer (espnService, priceCalculator, sentimentEngine)
- Data layer (static JSON scenario files in src/data/)
- Chrome extension integration
- Onboarding and first-trade flows
acceptance_criteria:
1. All user flows documented (Market browse, Player detail, Portfolio/Watchlist, Daily Mission, Leaderboard, Scenario switching, Onboarding)
2. Component and data-flow diagram produced (covering src/context/GameContext.jsx, src/services/, src/data/)
3. Must-keep behaviors explicitly listed (e.g., 3-scenario simulation, live-ticker, onboarding flow, chrome extension integration)
4. Known pain points and tech debt cataloged with severity

title: Tech Stack: Confirm Tooling and Validation Approach
description:
Lock down the validation and quality tooling that will guard the refactor loop. Current baseline: React 19, Vite 5.4, ESLint 9, no TypeScript, no tests, no E2E.

Decisions needed:
- TypeScript migration strategy (full migration vs. incremental .jsx -> .tsx)
- Test framework (Vitest is the natural fit given Vite)
- E2E tool (Playwright vs. Cypress) with rationale
- a11y validation approach (axe-core, eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y, or both)
- Whether to add Prettier or stick with ESLint formatting rules

Produce a list of concrete setup tasks that will be filed as child issues under the Issue Breakdown.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Decision recorded: TypeScript (full migration vs. incremental .jsx -> .tsx)
2. Test framework chosen and bootstrap task identified (Vitest likely, given Vite)
3. E2E tool chosen (Playwright vs. Cypress) with rationale
4. a11y validation approach decided (axe-core, eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y, or both)
5. List of setup tasks produced (e.g., add vitest config, add tsconfig.json, add playwright config)
6. All decisions documented with rationale
